source: Numerics/Sampling/MCMC/Support/MCMCDiagnostics.cs (MinimumSampleSize); no dedicated Test_MCMCDiagnostics.cs case exercises it (that test file only covers GelmanRubin -- see the mcmc_sampler-adjacent C++-only test_mcmc_extra.cpp for the GelmanRubin transcription), so every case here is curated. The `quantile=0.99, tolerance=0.01, probability=0.95` case is taken from the worked example in Numerics/docs/sampling/convergence-diagnostics.md (100-year-event quantile estimation), which documents the pre-rounding N ~= 380.42 -- the fixture asserts the actual Math.Round(N/100)*100 output.
reference: Numerics @ a2c4dbf. Deterministic scalar function of (quantile, tolerance, probability) via the Raftery-Lewis normal-approximation formula N = q(1-q) z((1+s)/2)^2 / r^2, rounded to the nearest 100. args = [quantile, tolerance, probability]. Every value was generated by running the case's inputs through tools/oracle_emitter --dump against the REAL C# MCMCDiagnostics class and confirming the printed actuals reproduce via verify_oracles.py.

assertions.source: curated via oracle_emitter --dump; cross-checked against the Numerics/docs/sampling/convergence-diagnostics.md worked example (pre-rounding N ~= 380.42, rounds to 400)
